definition: Occupational dust and fume exposures include inhaled particulates encountered in certain workplaces (e.g., mining, construction, manufacturing). These exposures are relevant to respiratory outcomes in population studies and can interact with baseline airway biology. GENARCH presents this as educational content.
name: Occupational Dust and Fume Exposure

references.title: Occupational exposures and COPD risk: evidence overview (review)

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Exposure often occurs during employment years; effects depend on duration and co-exposures such as smoking.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Inhaled dust can contribute to airway irritation and chronic inflammatory responses in cohort evidence.
